After you finish this page, click Next to continue to How to Avoid Making Mistakes with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling.

Week 1 | How to Avoid Making Mistakes with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling

Grammar, Punctuation, Spelling Excellent: Tips for Error Avoidance
To avoid making grammar, punctuation, and spelling mistakes in college writing, you can do a few things. First and foremost, always proofread your work before submitting it. Take the time to read through your essay carefully and make sure everything is correct.

One helpful tip is to read your essay out loud. This will help you catch errors that may have slipped through when reading silently. Additionally, try reading your essay backward sentence by sentence - this trick can help spot mistakes more easily.

Another way to avoid mistakes is to use online tools such as Grammarly, a free tool! Visit grammarly.comLinks to an external site. to sign up for a free account. You can also use your Word document's spelling/grammar tool to help catch errors. These tools can quickly scan your writing for grammatical errors, punctuation mistakes, and misspellings.
